title: The Menu Within the Menu

features.name: nested-chip
features.desc-on-touch: circuit board inside circuit board. Each layer contains a smaller version of itself. Files point to files that point to themselves. The pinouts lead to other boards leading to the same board, forever.

features.name: system-boot-unfinished
features.desc-on-touch: the boot sequence that never ends. Every version of it already exists in the previous version's documentation. A file corrupted to read: 'Boot_Cycle_Contains_Record_Of_All_Boot_Cycles_Including_This_One.txt'.

features.name: cursor-awaiting-input
features.desc-on-touch: the blinking has never stopped. The menu waits for a command that would navigate you to another menu, which is also this menu, which is also waiting for input.

features.name: option-that-returns-to-itself
features.desc-on-touch: select it and you are here. The text says 'select option to proceed' and the option leads here, and there is only this option. Recursion has become geography.

features.name: memory-trace
features.desc-on-touch: faint traces of menus long rendered, still visible in the substrate. All versions of the menu exist simultaneously in the phosphor. The layering is stable now; it learned to hold them all at once.

inscription: every navigation leads back to this screen; you have become part of the rendering

file.name: System_Menu_Contains_Reference_To_Itself_At_Every_Level_Verified.log
file.note: status: germinating — nesting is stable; the archive has learned to hold infinite versions inside itself without collapsing

exits.label: back to the room where all routes lead inward
into an even smaller menu that contains this one
towards a boot sequence that might not have started yet
